zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Ros於大約1361年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt26歲，母親Beatrice Stafford22歲。她於大約1378年11月25日與Reynold Grey II結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1414年在威爾士丹比爾盧享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Ros于大约1361年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt26岁，母亲Beatrice Stafford22岁。她于大约1378年11月25日与Reynold Grey II结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1414年在威尔士丹比爾盧享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Ros was born about 1361, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt, was 26 and her mother, Beatrice Stafford, was 22. She married Reynold Grey II about 25 November 1378. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died about 1414, in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Ros est née environ 1361, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Stafford, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Reynold Grey II environ 25 novembre 1378. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1414, à Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Ros um 1361 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt 26 und ihre Mutter Beatrice Stafford 22. Sie hat um 25. November 1378 Reynold Grey II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb um 1414 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ros è nata il circa 1361 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Beatrice Stafford, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Reynold Grey II circa 25 novembre 1378. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì circa 1414, nel Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, all'età di 55 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ros(이)가 대략 1361년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Stafford(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1378년 11월 25일 Reynold Grey II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1414년 웨일스 덴비그h실르 루스인에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Reynold Grey II aproximadamente 25 de novembro de 1378. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1414, em Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Ros родилась около 1361 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Stafford, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reynold Grey II около 25 ноября 1378 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла около 1414 г. в местности рутин, денбаишир, Уэльс в возрасте 55 лет.

Se casó con Reynold Grey II aproximadamente 25 de noviembre de 1378. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1414, en Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, a la edad de 55 años.
